Create an LLM Agent Node

Source: R/factory.R
add_llm_node.Rd

A convenience wrapper to instantiate an AgentLLMNode. Useful for functional-style DAG construction.

Usage

add_llm_node(id, role, driver, model = NULL, cli_opts = list(), ...)

Arguments

id

String. A unique identifier for the node within the DAG.

role

String. The system prompt or identity the LLM should assume (e.g., "Python Developer").

driver

AgentDriver. An R6 driver object (e.g., GeminiCLIDriver) that handles the LLM API.

model

String. Optional model name override (e.g., "gpt-4"). Defaults to driver default.

cli_opts

List. Optional parameters passed to the underlying CLI or API call.

...

Additional arguments. Passed directly to the AgentLLMNode$new() constructor. Useful for setting tools, prompt_builder, or output_path.

Value

An AgentLLMNode object.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# NOTE: Ensure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set in your .Renviron file for this example.

driver <- AnthropicAPIDriver$new()
node <- add_llm_node(
  id = "coder",
  role = "You are an expert R programmer.",
  driver = driver,
  model = "claude-3-sonnet",
  output_path = "scripts/generated_code.R"
)
} # }
